rationale: The cited paper is used in the model-development argument to justify why private labels across chains may be perceived as similar and thus capable of generating cross-brand learning. The citing paper specifically relies on it for claims that private labels often play a catch-up role versus national brands and that shared manufacturing can make private-label quality appear interchangeable. This is more than a passing mention because it supports a core conceptual premise behind the proposed spillover-learning mechanism, even though methods or data are not reused.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Steenkamp et al. (2010) as conceptual and empirical background for key assumptions about private-label similarity versus national brands. Those assumptions underpin the citing paper's cross-retailer private-label learning framework and interpretation of private label versus national brand competition.
